category_title: Students who repeat the teacher's text to avoid reading new material
minimal_context: The student and interventionist are taking turns reading 'Miss Nelson is missing!' during a one-on-one session.
dialogue:
TUTOR: I will read this line: 'The kids were being rude or silly.' Now it is your turn to read.

STUDENT: Was rude or that one.

TUTOR: That's the part I read. Try the next line.

STUDENT: I just said... was rude or silly.

TUTOR: What's the next one say?

STUDENT: You's gonna read the next one. I read this one.

options.A: Use a two-pocket folder wherein the left side is dedicated to reading tasks to be completed each session and the right to completed tasks.
options.B: Allow the student to select a book from several choices and use a constructivist approach to mediation, implementing scaffolds focusing on meaning and comprehension.
options.C: Record behavioral data in scatter plots and ABC charts after the shared reading session finishes each day during the one-on-one intervention.
options.D: Subdivide the shared reading sessions into 15-minute segments and introduce short breaks between the reading activities during the one-on-one intervention.
